title: Version 1.5.0 on quay.io ?
body:
On the docker registries on quay.io:
* https://quay.io/repository/containers/skopeo?tab=tags
* https://quay.io/repository/skopeo/stable?tab=tags
it seems the latest 1.5.0 build is not triggered to produce newest version 1.5.0 of the images.

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

@lsm5 I see 1.5.0 as stable on all branches in https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/?packages=skopeo ; any idea what is going wrong?
@TomSweeneyRedHat quay is probably something you own? ^

> @TomSweeneyRedHat quay is probably something you own? ^

@TomSweeneyRedHat also please remind me how I could get rights to quay image maintenance as well
This is probably in my boat-house, as much as I enjoy working with github-actions, I'll take a look...
...[The latest logs show builds for 1.4.1 being pushed](https://github.com/containers/skopeo/actions/runs/1433783264), so something is broken.
Oh, [looks like the images are based on F33](https://github.com/containers/skopeo/runs/4136128814?check_suite_focus=true), that's probably why.
@lsm5 is going to fix the Dockerfiles, they'll get automatically picked up in the daily build subsequent to the fix-merge.
